tasks.title: PyPI Name Pre-flight Check
tasks.description: Verify that the package name 'harpocrates' is available on PyPI. Hard gate — do not proceed to Phase 1 code if this fails.
tasks.details:
Run `pip index versions harpocrates`. Expected: 'ERROR: No matching distribution found'. If it returns version numbers the name is taken — all downstream tasks (publish.yml, README install command, pre-commit snippet, blog post, HN title) must be revised with the new name before any code is written.

Also check pypi.org/project/harpocrates in a browser as a secondary confirm. Record the result.
tasks.testStrategy: `pip index versions harpocrates` returns an error (no versions). If versions are returned, escalate immediately — do not proceed.

subtasks.title: Run pip index versions harpocrates
subtasks.description: Execute the pip index command to check PyPI name availability
subtasks.details:
Run: pip index versions harpocrates
Success: error output indicating no distribution found.
Failure: version list returned — name is taken.
subtasks.testStrategy: Command output contains 'ERROR' or 'No matching distribution'. No version numbers in output.

subtasks.title: Document result and confirm go/no-go
subtasks.description: Record the PyPI check result and make explicit go/no-go decision
subtasks.details:
If name is available: record confirmation, proceed with 'harpocrates' everywhere.
If name is taken: stop, choose alternative (harpocrates-scanner, harpocrates-ml, py-harpocrates), and update all downstream tasks before any implementation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Decision is documented and unambiguous. All downstream tasks reference the confirmed name.

tasks.title: Add package_data to pyproject.toml for Two-Stage Models
tasks.description: Bundle exactly the three two-stage ML model files in the wheel. Without this, pip install ships a regex-only fallback silently.
tasks.details:
Add to pyproject.toml:
```toml
[tool.setuptools.package-data]
"Harpocrates.ml.models" = ["stageA_xgboost.json", "stageB_lightgbm.txt", "two_stage_config.json"]
```

Do NOT use wildcards. Only these three two-stage files. The legacy models (ensemble_config.json, lightgbm_v1.txt, xgboost_v1.json) add 1.8MB of dead weight and must NOT be included.

Also remove `include_package_data = true` from pyproject.toml if it exists without a MANIFEST.in, as it provides false confidence without actually bundling anything.
tasks.testStrategy: Build with `python -m build --wheel`. Run `unzip -l dist/harpocrates-*.whl | grep -E 'models/.*\.(json|txt)'`. Must return exactly 3 lines matching the two-stage files. Legacy model names must NOT appear.

subtasks.title: Add [tool.setuptools.package-data] to pyproject.toml
subtasks.description: Add explicit package_data section listing the three two-stage model files
subtasks.details:
Edit pyproject.toml. Add after [tool.setuptools] or create it:
[tool.setuptools.package-data]
"Harpocrates.ml.models" = ["stageA_xgboost.json", "stageB_lightgbm.txt", "two_stage_config.json"]

Do not use wildcards. Do not include legacy model files.
subtasks.testStrategy: Read pyproject.toml and verify exact section exists with exactly 3 named files. No wildcards.

subtasks.title: Build wheel and verify model file inclusion
subtasks.description: Build the wheel distribution and confirm exactly 3 two-stage model files are present, legacy models are absent
subtasks.details:
Run: python -m build --wheel
Run: unzip -l dist/harpocrates-*.whl | grep -E 'models/'
Expect exactly 3 lines: stageA_xgboost.json, stageB_lightgbm.txt, two_stage_config.json.
Confirm absence of: ensemble_config.json, lightgbm_v1.txt, xgboost_v1.json.
subtasks.testStrategy: grep output has exactly 3 model file lines. wc -l confirms count. Legacy file names not present.

tasks.title: Add Dependency Upper Bounds to pyproject.toml
tasks.description: Add upper version bounds on xgboost and lightgbm to protect the published wheel against future serialization breakage.
tasks.details:
In pyproject.toml [project.dependencies], update or add:
  xgboost>=2.0.0,<4
  lightgbm>=4.0.0,<5

Rationale: xgboost 4+ may break the binary model format (stageA_xgboost.json). lightgbm 5+ may break stageB_lightgbm.txt. The upper bounds protect installed users without blocking compatible minor versions.

Do NOT pin exact versions — that breaks reproducibility on fresh installs.
tasks.testStrategy: Read pyproject.toml and verify both constraints are present with correct lower and upper bounds. Build wheel and inspect metadata: `unzip -p dist/*.whl '*/METADATA' | grep -E '(xgboost|lightgbm)'` shows correct requires lines.

subtasks.title: Update xgboost and lightgbm dependency specs in pyproject.toml
subtasks.description: Add >=lower,<upper bounds to both ML dependencies
subtasks.details:
Find the [project.dependencies] section. Update the xgboost line to: xgboost>=2.0.0,<4
Update or add lightgbm line to: lightgbm>=4.0.0,<5
Do not pin exact versions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Read pyproject.toml. Both lines contain correct range specs. No exact pins (`==`) on these packages.

subtasks.title: Verify wheel metadata reflects updated constraints
subtasks.description: Build wheel and confirm METADATA file shows correct dependency range specs
subtasks.details:
Run: python -m build --wheel
Run: unzip -p dist/*.whl '*/METADATA' | grep -E '(xgboost|lightgbm)'
Expect lines like: Requires-Dist: xgboost>=2.0.0,<4
subtasks.testStrategy: grep output shows both packages with >=lower,<upper bounds in METADATA. No exact pins.

tasks.title: Remove Exact Click Pin from requirements.txt
tasks.description: Remove or relax any exact version pin on click in requirements.txt to avoid install conflicts.
tasks.details:
Find the click entry in requirements.txt. If it reads `click==X.Y.Z`, change to `click>=8.0.0` (or whatever the minimum compatible version is) or remove it entirely if pyproject.toml already declares it.

An exact pin on click will cause pip conflicts when users have other tools that require a different click version. Pre-commit environments are especially sensitive to this.

If requirements.txt is only used for development, consider removing click from it entirely since pyproject.toml is the authoritative dependency spec.
tasks.testStrategy: Read requirements.txt and verify no `click==` exact pin exists. `pip install -e .` in a fresh venv completes without conflict.

subtasks.title: Locate and remove click exact pin
subtasks.description: Find click in requirements.txt and remove or relax the exact pin
subtasks.details:
Run: grep -n 'click' requirements.txt
If exact pin found (click==X.Y.Z): change to click>=8.0.0 or delete the line if pyproject.toml already has it.
Check pyproject.toml [project.dependencies] for click — if it's there, delete the line from requirements.txt to avoid duplication.
subtasks.testStrategy: grep -n 'click==' requirements.txt returns 0 results. No exact pin remains.

subtasks.title: Verify clean install in fresh venv
subtasks.description: Confirm pip install -e . succeeds without dependency conflict after pin removal
subtasks.details:
Create a fresh venv: python -m venv /tmp/test-click-venv
Activate and run: pip install -e .
Confirm no conflict errors. Run: harpocrates --help to confirm CLI entry point works.
subtasks.testStrategy: pip install -e . exits 0. harpocrates --help runs and shows usage without error.

tasks.title: Create tests/test_packaging.py with ML-bundled assertion
tasks.description: Write a packaging smoke test that builds the wheel, installs it in a clean venv, and asserts the ML pipeline is active (not regex fallback).
tasks.details:
Create tests/test_packaging.py. The test must:
1. Build the wheel via subprocess (`python -m build --wheel`)
2. Create a fresh venv via subprocess (`python -m venv /tmp/test-harpo-venv`)
3. Pip install from wheel (NOT from source tree): `pip install dist/harpocrates-*.whl`
4. Run CLI against tests/fixtures/test_tokens.py (which already exists and contains a detectable pattern)
5. Assert `'Two-stage ML verification enabled'` appears in stdout
6. Assert exit code is non-zero (at least one finding detected)

CRITICAL: Use the actual CLI default threshold (`--ml-threshold 0.19`), NOT a permissive test-only threshold. Exit-code-only assertion would pass even on a broken wheel.

CRITICAL: This test installs from the wheel, not from source. Mixing venv creation into pytest is fine here — this is a packaging integration test, not a unit test.

If any AWS regex patterns appear in test fixtures, use the looser form `\s*[:=]\s*["']?` (not `{0,5}`) to avoid tight anchors that miss real-world formatting variations.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pytest tests/test_packaging.py -v`. Test must PASS after task 2 (package_data) is done and FAIL if package_data is reverted (models missing from wheel). The assertion on 'Two-stage ML verification enabled' is the correctness gate.

subtasks.title: Write test_packaging.py with venv-isolation smoke test
subtasks.description: Create the packaging test that builds wheel, installs in clean venv, runs CLI, asserts ML pipeline active
subtasks.details:
File: tests/test_packaging.py

Test flow:
1. subprocess.run(['python', '-m', 'build', '--wheel'], check=True)
2. Find wheel: glob('dist/harpocrates-*.whl')[0]
3. venv_path = tempfile.mkdtemp() + '/venv'; subprocess.run(['python', '-m', 'venv', venv_path], check=True)
4. pip = venv_path + '/bin/pip'; subprocess.run([pip, 'install', wheel_path], check=True)
5. harpo = venv_path + '/bin/harpocrates'
6. result = subprocess.run([harpo, 'scan', 'tests/fixtures/test_tokens.py', '--ml-threshold', '0.19'], capture_output=True, text=True)
7. assert 'Two-stage ML verification enabled' in result.stdout
8. assert result.returncode != 0  # found at least one secret

Handle teardown of the temp venv. Mark test with @pytest.mark.slow or @pytest.mark.integration if needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: pytest tests/test_packaging.py passes with package_data in place. Fails if package_data removed.

subtasks.title: Verify test fails correctly without package_data
subtasks.description: Temporarily remove package_data, run the test, confirm it fails, then restore
subtasks.details:
To validate the test is a real gate:
1. Temporarily comment out [tool.setuptools.package-data] in pyproject.toml
2. Run python -m build --wheel
3. Run pytest tests/test_packaging.py
4. Confirm test FAILS (ML message not in stdout)
5. Restore pyproject.toml and re-run — test must PASS

This confirms the test actually catches the breakage, not just a false green.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test FAILS without package_data, PASSES with package_data. Both states verified.

tasks.title: CLI paths: List[Path] + zero-file early return + UnicodeDecodeError handling
tasks.description: Update the CLI scan command to accept multiple file paths, return exit 0 on zero arguments, and gracefully skip binary/unreadable files with a warning.
tasks.details:
In Harpocrates/cli.py, update the `scan` command:

1. Change the `path` argument (single) to `paths: List[Path]` (variadic, zero or more).
   Click syntax: `@click.argument('paths', nargs=-1, type=click.Path(exists=True, path_type=Path))`

2. Zero-file early return: if `len(paths) == 0`, print nothing (or optional 'No files to scan.') and `sys.exit(0)`.

3. Per-file UnicodeDecodeError handling: for each path in paths, wrap the file read in:
   ```python
   try:
       content = path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   except UnicodeDecodeError:
       click.echo(f'[WARNING] Skipping binary/unreadable file: {path}', err=True)
       continue
   ```
   This ensures binary files (images, compiled assets) never crash the pre-commit hook.

4. Also catch OSError/PermissionError per file for robustness (pre-commit may pass files the process can't read).

Do NOT change Stage A or Stage B thresholds. They stay at training defaults (0.1/0.9). This task is CLI plumbing only.
tasks.testStrategy: `harpocrates scan a.txt b.txt c.env` processes all three. `harpocrates scan` (zero args) exits 0. Passing a binary file (e.g., a compiled .pyc) prints WARNING to stderr and exits without crashing. All verified by test_cli.py task 7.

subtasks.title: Update scan command argument to accept List[Path]
subtasks.description: Change single path argument to variadic paths using click nargs=-1
subtasks.testStrategy: `harpocrates scan a.txt b.txt` processes both files. `harpocrates scan single.txt` still works.

subtasks.title: Add zero-file early return
subtasks.description: If paths is empty, exit 0 immediately without error
subtasks.details:
At the top of the scan function body, after checking paths:
  if not paths:
      sys.exit(0)

This is the pre-commit no-staged-files case. Must exit 0, not print usage error.
subtasks.testStrategy: `harpocrates scan` with no arguments exits with code 0. No error message printed.

subtasks.title: Add per-file UnicodeDecodeError and OSError handling
subtasks.description: Wrap each file read in try/except to skip binary or unreadable files gracefully
subtasks.testStrategy: Passing a .pyc or binary file: WARNING to stderr, exit code reflects only readable-file findings. No traceback.

tasks.title: Add Three New Multi-File Test Cases to tests/test_cli.py
tasks.description: Add test cases covering: multi-file scan, zero-file scan (exit 0), and binary/unreadable file graceful skip.
tasks.details:
In tests/test_cli.py (or create if not present), add three test functions:

1. `test_scan_multiple_files`: Call `harpocrates scan a.txt b.txt c.env` (use fixtures with at least one containing a detectable secret). Assert all three files processed, correct exit code.

2. `test_scan_zero_files`: Call `harpocrates scan` with no arguments. Assert exit code 0. Assert no error message in stdout.

3. `test_scan_skips_binary_file`: Create a temp binary file (write non-UTF-8 bytes). Call `harpocrates scan <binary_file> <clean_text_file>`. Assert: WARNING appears in stderr, clean_text_file IS processed, no crash, exit code reflects clean file only.

Use Click's `CliRunner` for isolation. Use `pytest tmp_path` fixture for temp files.

Do NOT mock the detector or ML pipeline in these tests — they test real CLI plumbing.
tasks.testStrategy: `pytest tests/test_cli.py -v` passes all three new cases. Each case is clearly named and asserts the specific behavior it covers.

subtasks.title: Add test_scan_multiple_files
subtasks.description: Test that harpocrates scan processes multiple file arguments
subtasks.details: Use CliRunner. Create 3 temp files (a.txt, b.txt, c.env) at least one with a detectable pattern from tests/fixtures. Invoke CLI with all 3 paths. Assert exit code matches expected (non-zero if secret found).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test passes. CLI processes all 3 files without error.

subtasks.title: Add test_scan_zero_files
subtasks.description: Test that harpocrates scan with no arguments exits 0
subtasks.details: Use CliRunner. Invoke `scan` with empty args list. Assert result.exit_code == 0. Assert result.output is empty or contains no error text.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test passes. exit_code is 0. No error string in output.

subtasks.title: Add test_scan_skips_binary_file
subtasks.description: Test that binary files are skipped with WARNING, not crash
subtasks.details:
Use tmp_path fixture. Create binary file: `binary = tmp_path / 'test.bin'; binary.write_bytes(b'\x80\x81\x82\xff')`.
Create clean text file: `clean = tmp_path / 'clean.txt'; clean.write_text('x = 1')`.
Invoke: `harpocrates scan <binary> <clean>`.
Assert: '[WARNING]' in result.stderr (or output with mix_stderr=False). No traceback in output. exit_code is not 2 (not a crash).
subtasks.testStrategy: WARNING string present in stderr. No exception traceback. Clean file was processed.

tasks.title: git mv github/ .github/
tasks.description: Rename the misnamed github/ directory to .github/ so GitHub Actions workflows are recognized by GitHub CI.
tasks.details:
Run: git mv github/ .github/

This is required for GitHub to recognize the workflows directory. The current name `github/` is invisible to GitHub Actions.

After the mv:
1. Verify `.github/workflows/` exists and contains the expected workflow files.
2. Commit with message: `0.1-8: rename github/ to .github/ for GitHub Actions recognition`
3. Push to main.
4. Verify GitHub Actions tab shows the workflows.

If any workflow files currently reference paths relative to `github/`, update them to `.github/` after the move.
tasks.testStrategy: `ls .github/workflows/` shows existing workflow files. `git status` shows the rename cleanly. After push, GitHub repository shows Actions tab populated.

subtasks.title: Run git mv and verify directory structure
subtasks.description: Execute git mv github/ .github/ and confirm all workflow files moved correctly
subtasks.details:
Run: git mv github/ .github/
Run: ls .github/workflows/ to confirm files present.
Check for any files inside that reference 'github/' path and update them.
subtasks.testStrategy: .github/workflows/ exists with all files. github/ no longer exists. git status shows rename.

subtasks.title: Commit and push rename
subtasks.description: Commit the directory rename and push to main
subtasks.details:
git add -A
git commit -m '0.1-8: rename github/ to .github/ for GitHub Actions'
git push origin main

Verify on GitHub.com that the Actions tab is now visible.
subtasks.testStrategy: GitHub repository Actions tab is visible after push.

tasks.title: Create .pre-commit-hooks.yaml at Repo Root
tasks.description: Add the pre-commit hooks definition file so users can reference this repo in their .pre-commit-config.yaml.
tasks.details:
Create `.pre-commit-hooks.yaml` at the repo root with exactly:
```yaml
- id: harpocrates
  name: Harpocrates secrets scan
  entry: harpocrates scan
  language: python
  types_or: [text]
  pass_filenames: true
```

CRITICAL: Do NOT use `types: [python]` — Harpocrates scans all file types (env files, YAML, shell scripts, config files). Use `types_or: [text]` or omit the type filter entirely.

`pass_filenames: true` is required so pre-commit passes staged filenames to the `scan` command as arguments.

After creating the file, test the hook end-to-end in a throwaway repo:
1. Create a temp repo: `mkdir /tmp/test-hook-repo && cd /tmp/test-hook-repo && git init`
2. Create a `.pre-commit-config.yaml` pointing to the local harpocrates repo
3. Run `pre-commit run harpocrates --files secret.env` against a file with a fake secret
4. Confirm it detects and fails (non-zero exit)
5. Run against a clean file, confirm exit 0
tasks.testStrategy: `.pre-commit-hooks.yaml` exists at repo root. Hook id is `harpocrates`. `types_or: [text]` present (NOT `types: [python]`). End-to-end test: fake secret detected, clean file passes.

subtasks.title: Create .pre-commit-hooks.yaml at repo root
subtasks.description: Write the hooks definition file with correct id, entry, language, and type filter
subtasks.details:
Create file at repo root: .pre-commit-hooks.yaml
Content as specified. Verify types_or: [text] is present, NOT types: [python].
subtasks.testStrategy: File exists at repo root. types_or present, not types.

subtasks.title: End-to-end hook test in throwaway repo
subtasks.description: Test the hook works in a fresh git repo using pre-commit
subtasks.details:
1. mkdir /tmp/test-hook-repo && cd /tmp/test-hook-repo && git init
2. Create .pre-commit-config.yaml referencing local harpocrates path
3. Create secret.env with fake AWS key pattern
4. git add secret.env && pre-commit run harpocrates --files secret.env
5. Assert non-zero exit (secret found)
6. Assert exit 0 on clean file
subtasks.testStrategy: pre-commit exits non-zero on file with fake secret. Exits 0 on clean file. No crash on binary file.

tasks.title: Create .github/workflows/publish.yml with PyPI Trusted Publishing + Smoke Step
tasks.description: GitHub Actions workflow that builds, tests, and publishes to PyPI on tag push using OIDC trusted publishing (no stored token).
tasks.details:
Create `.github/workflows/publish.yml`. Key requirements:

- Trigger: `on: push: tags: ['v*']`
- Permissions: `id-token: write` (required for OIDC)
- Steps: checkout → setup-python 3.12 → pip install build → python -m build → smoke step → pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1

Smoke step installs from the built wheel (NOT from source) and runs:
  `pip install dist/*.whl && harpocrates scan tests/fixtures/test_tokens.py`

This catches a broken wheel before it reaches PyPI.

Use `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` for OIDC trusted publishing. No `PYPI_TOKEN` secret required.

Pre-requisite (one-time manual step on PyPI): configure Trusted Publishing at pypi.org/manage/account/publishing/ with owner=Skipa776, repo=Harpocrates, workflow=publish.yml. Document this in a comment inside the workflow file.
tasks.testStrategy: Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/publish.yml. Triggers on v* tags only. Has `id-token: write`. Smoke step installs from dist/*.whl. Uses pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1.

subtasks.title: Write .github/workflows/publish.yml
subtasks.description: Create the publish workflow YAML with OIDC trusted publishing and smoke step
subtasks.details:
Create .github/workflows/publish.yml. Verify:
- Triggers on push.tags: ['v*']
- permissions: id-token: write
- Build step: python -m build
- Smoke step: pip install dist/*.whl (not from source)
- Publish: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
- Comment inside file documenting the one-time PyPI trusted publishing setup requirement
subtasks.testStrategy: YAML is syntactically valid. All required steps present. id-token permission present.

subtasks.title: Document PyPI trusted publishing setup requirement
subtasks.description: Ensure the one-time manual PyPI configuration step is documented before the first tag push
subtasks.details:
On PyPI.org, configure Trusted Publishing:
  pypi.org/manage/account/publishing/ → Add Publisher: GitHub, owner=Skipa776, repo=Harpocrates, workflow=publish.yml

This must be done BEFORE pushing the v0.1.0 tag. Without it the OIDC publish will return 403.
Document in publish.yml as a comment and/or in the README publishing section.
subtasks.testStrategy: Documentation is visible in publish.yml comments. The first tag push does not fail due to missing trusted publishing config.

tasks.title: README Rewrite: Hero, AI Stat, Case Study Table, Install, Pre-commit Snippet
tasks.description: Rewrite the README hero section to lead with the AI leak stat, include the case study comparison table, and provide clear install and pre-commit setup instructions.
tasks.details:
Rewrite README.md. Structure:

1. **Hero**: Lead with the fear driver — AI coding tools write code at 2× the secret-leak rate (3.2% vs 1.6%). 29M secrets exposed in 2025, up 34% YoY. $87k overnight AWS bill.

2. **The gap**: Regex scanners miss credentials with ambiguous variable names. Harpocrates catches what regex can't see.

3. **Case study table**: 5 rows from comparison/case_study.md — TruffleHog miss, Harpocrates catch. Show: variable name, secret type, TruffleHog result, Harpocrates result.

4. **Install**:
   ```
   pip install harpocrates
   harpocrates scan .
   ```

5. **Pre-commit snippet**:
   ```yaml
   repos:
     - repo: https://github.com/Skipa776/Harpocrates
       rev: v0.1.0
       hooks:
         - id: harpocrates
   ```

6. **False-negative callout**: one-line note linking to Issues (wired in task 12).

Keep focused. No architecture diagrams, no API docs, no training instructions in the hero flow.
tasks.testStrategy: README opens with AI stat. Case study table present with 5 rows. Install command present. Pre-commit snippet with v0.1.0 rev. False-negative callout visible.

subtasks.title: Read comparison/case_study.md and extract key table rows
subtasks.description: Identify the 5 most impactful TruffleHog-miss / Harpocrates-catch examples
subtasks.details: Read comparison/case_study.md. Select 5 rows where TruffleHog returns 0 findings and Harpocrates detects the secret. Capture: variable name, secret type, TruffleHog result (miss), Harpocrates result (catch).
subtasks.testStrategy: 5 rows selected. Each clearly demonstrates the ambiguous-variable-name gap.

subtasks.title: Rewrite README.md with full structure
subtasks.description: Write the new README content following the 6-section structure
subtasks.details: Structure: fear hook → gap statement → case study table (5 rows) → install command → pre-commit snippet → false-negative callout → brief how-it-works (3-phase pipeline, 1 paragraph). Do not pad.
subtasks.testStrategy: README starts with AI stat sentence. All 6 structural elements present. Pre-commit snippet uses v0.1.0.

tasks.title: Create false-negative GitHub Issues Label + README Callout

tasks.details:

   `gh label create false-negative --color 'e11d48' --description 'Harpocrates missed a secret that should have been detected' --repo Skipa776/Harpocrates`

2. Add to README.md (after case study section):
   > **Found a secret Harpocrates missed?** [Open an issue with the `false-negative` label](https://github.com/Skipa776/Harpocrates/issues/new?labels=false-negative). This is the most valuable feedback you can give.

One labeled false-negative issue is the strongest success metric for Release 0.1.0.

subtasks.testStrategy: README contains callout. URL includes correct repo path and labels param.

tasks.title: Stranger Install Test (Out-of-Project Human Validation)
tasks.description: Have a person not involved in the project run pip install harpocrates in a clean venv and scan a file with a fake secret. If it fails, do not post anywhere.
tasks.details:
This is a human gate, not an automated test.

Prepare instructions for the tester:
1. `python -m venv /tmp/harpo-stranger-test && source /tmp/harpo-stranger-test/bin/activate`
2. `pip install harpocrates`
3. `echo 'AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE0000000000000000000a"' > test.env`
4. `harpocrates scan test.env`
5. Expect: non-zero exit, finding in output, 'Two-stage ML verification enabled' in stdout

Pass: stranger on clean machine gets tool working end-to-end without consulting source code.
Fail: any install error, import error, model-not-found error, crash.

One shot at HN — if a stranger can't run it, don't post.
tasks.testStrategy: Stranger reports successful scan with ML enabled. Zero errors. Document tester's OS, Python version, and exact output.

subtasks.title: Prepare stranger test instructions
subtasks.description: Write self-contained 5-step test script for external tester
subtasks.details: Write verbatim commands + expected outputs. Tester needs zero prior knowledge of the project. Include: venv creation, pip install, test file creation, scan command, expected output.
subtasks.testStrategy: Instructions are self-contained. An unfamiliar developer can follow them without asking questions.

subtasks.title: Run stranger test and document result
subtasks.description: External person executes instructions and reports outcome
subtasks.details: Collect from tester: OS, Python version, pip install output, scan output, exit code, whether 'Two-stage ML verification enabled' appeared. If any step fails, treat as release blocker — fix before task 14.
subtasks.testStrategy: Tester reports success. All 5 steps pass. 'Two-stage ML verification enabled' in output.

tasks.title: Cut v0.1.0 Tag, Verify publish.yml Runs Green, Confirm PyPI Live
tasks.description: Push the v0.1.0 tag to trigger the publish workflow. Verify green. Confirm pip install harpocrates works from PyPI.
tasks.details:
Pre-conditions (all must be true before this task):
- All tasks 1–12 merged to main
- CI green
- Stranger test (task 13) passed
- PyPI trusted publishing configured on pypi.org

Steps:
1. `git status` — no uncommitted changes
2. `git tag v0.1.0`
3. `git push origin v0.1.0`
4. Watch GitHub Actions publish.yml run to completion
5. Confirm all steps green (build → smoke → publish)
6. Wait ~2-5 minutes for PyPI propagation
7. Fresh venv: `pip install harpocrates` → version 0.1.0
8. `harpocrates scan tests/fixtures/test_tokens.py` → 'Two-stage ML verification enabled'

Do NOT push tag speculatively. Once pushed, the package is public.
tasks.testStrategy: publish.yml run is green. `pip index versions harpocrates` returns 0.1.0. Fresh venv install + scan shows ML enabled.

subtasks.title: Verify all pre-conditions are met
subtasks.description: Confirm tasks 1–12 merged, CI green, stranger test passed, PyPI trusted publishing configured
subtasks.details:
Checklist:
- git log: all task commits on main
- .github/workflows/publish.yml exists
- pyproject.toml version = '0.1.0'
- Stranger test documented as passed
- PyPI trusted publishing confirmed
subtasks.testStrategy: All pre-conditions explicitly verified before tag push.

subtasks.title: Push v0.1.0 tag and monitor publish.yml
subtasks.description: Cut the release tag and watch the publish workflow run
subtasks.details:
git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0
Watch GitHub Actions → publish.yml → latest run
All steps must be green. If any step fails: do NOT proceed to tasks 15/16.
subtasks.testStrategy: publish.yml shows green checkmark for all steps.

subtasks.title: Verify PyPI listing and fresh venv install
subtasks.description: Confirm pip install harpocrates installs v0.1.0 with ML pipeline active
subtasks.details:
python -m venv /tmp/post-publish-verify && source activate
pip install harpocrates
pip show harpocrates | grep Version  # must be 0.1.0
harpocrates scan tests/fixtures/test_tokens.py
Assert 'Two-stage ML verification enabled' in output.
subtasks.testStrategy: pip show version is 0.1.0. Scan output contains ML enabled message.

tasks.title: Write and Publish Blog Post on dev.to
tasks.description: Write and publish the launch blog post targeting ~1,000 words on dev.to.
tasks.details:
Platform: dev.to
Target length: ~1,000 words
Title: 'AI coding tools are doubling your secret leak rate — here’s how to catch what TruffleHog misses'

Structure:
1. Fear hook: $87k AWS bill overnight. 29M secrets exposed in 2025.
2. The mechanism: AI coding tools write code at 2× the secret-leak rate of human-written code.
3. The gap: regex scanners miss credentials with ambiguous variable names. Show 2-3 concrete examples from case_study.md.
4. How Harpocrates works: 3-phase (regex → entropy → ML). One sentence each.
5. Install + pre-commit setup: verbatim commands.
6. Case study results table.
7. CTA: install, try it, report false negatives (link to GitHub Issues with false-negative label).

Tags: security, devops, python, machinelearning.

Do NOT publish until task 14 is confirmed green. PyPI must be live when the post goes up.
tasks.testStrategy: Post is live on dev.to with public URL. Title matches spec. Contains working install command. Contains case study table. Contains false-negative Issues link. ~1,000 words.

subtasks.title: Draft blog post content
subtasks.description: Write the full ~1,000 word post following the 7-section structure
subtasks.details: Draft in Markdown. 7 sections: fear hook → AI mechanism → gap → how it works → install → case study table → CTA. Extract case study rows from comparison/case_study.md. Review for accuracy.
subtasks.testStrategy: Draft is ~1,000 words. All 7 sections present. Install command is correct. Case study data is accurate.

subtasks.title: Publish on dev.to and verify live
subtasks.description: Post the article on dev.to and confirm it is publicly accessible
subtasks.details: Log in to dev.to. Create new post. Add tags: security, devops, python, machinelearning. Publish (not draft). Copy public URL. Verify rendering: table displays, code blocks formatted.
subtasks.testStrategy: Post URL returns 200. Title, install command, case study table all visible. Tags applied.

tasks.title: Submit Show HN
tasks.description: Submit to Hacker News Show HN with the finalized title. One shot — do not resubmit.
tasks.details:
Submit to: news.ycombinator.com/submit
Type: Show HN

Title options (resolve OQ-1 — user's call on launch day):
- Option A (AI angle): 'Show HN: Harpocrates — AI coding tools double secret leak rates, here’s the fix'
- Option B (comparison angle): 'Show HN: Harpocrates — ML secrets scanner that catches what TruffleHog misses'

URL: dev.to blog post (preferred) or https://github.com/Skipa776/Harpocrates

Show HN body (≤3 paragraphs):
- What it does (1 sentence)
- The gap vs TruffleHog/Gitleaks with concrete numbers from case study
- How to try it (install command + pre-commit snippet)

Submit at peak HN time: 8–10am US Eastern weekday.
Do NOT submit until blog post (task 15) is live.
One shot. Do not resubmit.
tasks.testStrategy: HN submission is live at news.ycombinator.com/item?id=XXX. Show HN tag visible. Body contains install command. Title resolves OQ-1.

subtasks.title: Finalize HN title (resolve OQ-1)
subtasks.description: User makes final decision on AI-angle vs comparison-angle title
subtasks.details: Present both options to user. Get explicit confirmation before submitting. Record decision.
subtasks.testStrategy: Title confirmed by user. Decision documented.

subtasks.title: Write Show HN body
subtasks.description: Draft the ≤3 paragraph HN post body
subtasks.details: Body: what it does (1 sentence) + gap with concrete finding count from case study + how to try it (pip install + pre-commit snippet). No marketing fluff.
subtasks.testStrategy: Body is ≤3 paragraphs. Contains concrete number from case study. Contains install command.

subtasks.title: Submit to HN at peak time
subtasks.description: Submit the Show HN post at 8–10am US Eastern on a weekday
subtasks.details: news.ycombinator.com/submit. Show HN. Enter title, URL, body. Submit. Record HN item ID. Monitor early comments.
subtasks.testStrategy: HN submission live with correct title, URL, and body. Submitted at peak time.
